authority: Industry code; compliance is a condition of the generation, transmission, interconnector and distribution licences
summary: The technical rulebook for the GB transmission system and all parties connected to or operating with it. Governs the day-to-day planning, operation, and connection of the national electricity transmission system. All TOs, large generators (≥100 MW in E&W / lower thresholds elsewhere), interconnector operators, large demand customers, and DSOs must comply.
scope: Connection Conditions (CC) — technical requirements to connect to the transmission system
Planning Code (PC) — long-term planning data exchange
Operating Code (OC) — real-time operations, testing, outage planning, BM instructions
Balancing Code (BC) — balancing services, frequency control, reserve
Data Registration Code (DRC) — what data must be submitted and when
European Connection Conditions (ECC) — aligned with EU Network Codes for Grid Connection (RfG, DCC, HVDC)

keyParts.name: CC — Connection Conditions
keyParts.detail: Fault ride-through, reactive capability, frequency range, voltage control, power quality, communications.

keyParts.name: PC — Planning Code
keyParts.detail: Week 24 (W24) data submissions; long-term outage and operational planning data.

keyParts.name: OC1–OC11 — Operating Code
keyParts.detail: Outage planning, demand forecasting, black start, system test procedures, monitoring & control.

keyParts.name: BC1–BC3 — Balancing Code
keyParts.detail: Gate closure, Balancing Mechanism, imbalance data, final physical notifications.

keyParts.name: CUSC / Bilateral Agreement interface
keyParts.detail: Works alongside CUSC: Grid Code = technical; CUSC = commercial.

keyParts.name: Modification process
keyParts.detail: Grid Code Review Panel — changes proposed via GC modifications, decided by Ofgem.
